executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 306,342.76 inc VAT (282,043.20 ex VAT) for the week ending 20 June 2026.
Overall margin of 16.94% is recorded across the store, with significant distortion caused by anomalous department-level cost of sales figures that require investigation.
Total waste of 5,407.66 represents a waste percentage of 1.92% against ex-VAT sales, which is above acceptable thresholds and requires immediate attention.

Multiple fresh departments — Beef, Lamb, Pork, Poultry, and Cigarettes — are reporting negative cost of sales, indicating stock valuation or goods-in data errors that must be reconciled before the week closes.
Produce is the highest waste contributor at 1,684.83, followed by Fish at 1,291.08 and Provisions at 701.94, collectively accounting for 3,677.85 or 68% of total store waste.

The TOTAL department block shows a combined negative COGS of -15,959.48 against sales of 98,771.68, which is not commercially credible and points to missing purchase data across all fresh departments.
Deli scan margin of 39.81% stands out as the strongest theoretical margin in the store, though actual reported margin of 93.3% suggests a stock or purchase data issue there also.
Provisions reported a near-100% actual margin of 99.83% against a scan margin of 19.87%, which is another clear data integrity concern requiring urgent investigation.
biggest_wins: Produce delivered the highest sales contribution in the fresh block at 33,320.81, with a reported margin of 75.12% against a scan margin of 19.02%, though the absence of a closing stock figure means the true margin cannot be confirmed.
Deli achieved sales of 7,714.71 with a scan margin of 39.81%, the highest theoretical margin in the store, indicating strong product mix and pricing discipline in that department.
Poultry posted sales of 10,565.04 with a scan margin of 16.77%, performing solidly within the fresh protein category and representing a meaningful sales contribution.

Fish recorded sales of 4,645.86 and is one of the few departments showing a positive and broadly credible cost of sales figure of 72.25, suggesting stock management in that area is reasonably accurate.
Deli maintained a low closing stock of 3,609.59 versus an opening of 4,126.51, indicating good stock rotation and reduced exposure to end-of-week waste in that department.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes is reporting a margin of 295.94% with a negative COGS of -13,145.56, driven by closing stock of 127,896.58 exceeding opening stock of 114,751.02 with no purchases recorded. This is a critical data error and must be escalated to the office immediately.
Beef shows a margin of 173.67% with negative COGS of -8,185.27, again with no purchases recorded. Given sales of 11,111.08, this figure is commercially impossible and indicates missing goods-in data.
Lamb and Pork are similarly reporting impossible margins of 211.41% and 160.78% respectively, both with negative cost of sales due to absent purchase figures.
Provisions waste of 701.94 is disproportionately high for a department with an opening stock of only 247.62, suggesting significant over-ordering or short-dated product arriving from suppliers.
Fish waste of 1,291.08 against sales of 4,645.86 equates to a waste rate of approximately 27.8% within the department, which is unsustainable and requires an immediate ordering and display review.

Produce waste of 1,684.83 is the single largest waste line in the store. Combined with a missing closing stock figure, the true margin for Produce cannot be calculated, leaving a significant blind spot in the P&L.
Total store waste of 5,407.66 at 1.92% of ex-VAT sales is above the acceptable threshold and is being driven by fresh and perishable categories across the board.

Bacon and Loose SRP recorded a scan margin of only 10.99%, the lowest in the store among fresh departments, indicating a potential pricing or shrinkage issue that should be reviewed against current promotional activity.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Margin of 295.94% is not credible. Closing stock of 127,896.58 versus opening of 114,751.02 with zero purchases recorded has produced a negative COGS of -13,145.56. Purchases must be posted and stock count verified urgently.
Beef: Negative COGS of -8,185.27 and margin of 173.67% against scan of 20.37% confirms missing purchase data. Sales of 11,111.08 are solid but the P&L entry is unusable in its current form.
Lamb: Margin of 211.41% versus scan of 26.34% confirms the same missing purchases issue. Sales of 1,119.07 are modest but the data must be corrected.
Pork: Margin of 160.78% versus scan of 23.16% with negative COGS of -765.83 reflects unposted purchases. Sales of 1,259.90 are within normal range.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Negative COGS of -273.77 and margin of 135.88% versus a scan of only 10.99% highlights both missing purchases and a concern around the low scan margin which should be investigated independently.
Poultry: Margin of 111.91% versus scan of 16.77% indicates missing purchases. Sales of 10,565.04 are strong and waste of 48.27 is well controlled relative to turnover.

Fish: One of the more credible entries with a positive COGS of 72.25 and margin of 98.44%, but waste of 1,291.08 against sales of 4,645.86 is a serious concern requiring immediate ordering and date-checking action.

Produce: Sales of 33,320.81 are strong and represent the largest fresh department. However, missing closing stock means the margin of 75.12% is unverified. Waste of 1,684.83 must be addressed with tighter ordering.

Deli: Scan margin of 39.81% is the highest in the store. Actual reported margin of 93.3% is inflated and likely reflects a stock or purchase data issue. Waste of 484.54 should be monitored. Stock rotation appears reasonable given closing stock reduction.

Provisions: Reported margin of 99.83% against scan of 19.87% is not credible and indicates a posting error. Waste of 701.94 against an opening stock of only 247.62 is extremely high and requires an immediate supplier and ordering review.

Total Fresh Block: Combined purchases figure of 0.00 across all departments confirms a systemic failure to post purchase invoices for the week. This must be resolved before any meaningful P&L analysis can be completed.
action_plan: Escalate missing purchase data immediately to the store accountant and department managers — all fresh and grocery departments show zero purchases recorded, rendering the P&L figures unusable for margin analysis until corrected.
Reconcile Cigarettes stock count against the closing figure of 127,896.58 and post all outstanding purchase invoices to correct the impossible margin of 295.94% before the weekly P&L is finalised.

Conduct a full Fish department waste and ordering review. Waste of 1,291.08 against sales of 4,645.86 equates to an approximate internal waste rate of 27.8% and is not sustainable. Reduce order quantities and increase date-checking frequency immediately.
Obtain and post the Produce closing stock figure — this is currently missing, making it impossible to calculate a verified GP% for the highest-volume fresh department in the store.

Investigate Provisions waste of 701.94, which significantly exceeds the department opening stock of 247.62. Review supplier deliveries, product dating on arrival, and chilled storage conditions to identify the root cause.
Review Produce ordering patterns to address waste of 1,684.83. Implement a daily waste log for the Produce department and set a target waste reduction of 20% for the following week.

Investigate the Bacon and Loose SRP scan margin of 10.99%, the lowest theoretical margin in the store. Review current pricing, promotions, and shrinkage levels to determine whether pricing corrections or wastage controls are required.

Ensure all department managers complete a physical stock count reconciliation for week ending 20 June 2026 and submit closing stock figures before the P&L is submitted, with particular urgency on Produce.

Review Deli waste of 484.54 and bread and cakes waste of 446.38 in the context of current production scheduling. Reduce late-day production volumes and implement a markdown trigger for product approaching end of shelf life.
Dairy waste of 377.28 and frozen waste of 219.25, while not the largest lines, should be reviewed against ordering levels and storage compliance to prevent escalation in subsequent weeks.

Schedule a meeting with the fresh food buyer to review Fish and Provisions supplier performance, focusing on product dating and delivery frequency, with a view to reducing short-dated stock arriving into the store.

Set a store-wide waste reduction target for the week ending 27 June 2026, with a maximum acceptable waste percentage of 1.60% against ex-VAT sales, and assign department-level targets to each fresh manager.
